original_reasoning: هدن=calm/quiet; ἁδινόν=thick/deep/plentiful. Possible semantic link via stillness
Tocharian; takhar place name. Historically Tocharoi=Central Asian people; plausible Arabic borrowing.
Being filled with grief/suppressed vs 'from the depths of the heart'
Suppression/being filled with grief vs 'from the depths of one's heart' - shared emotional depth

Arabic basala = brave/fierce/courageous warrior (basil = bold), also 'forbidden/lawful'; Greek Basileios = royal, kingly (from basileus 'king'). The Arabic 'brave warrior/leader' and Greek 'kingly/ruler' share the conceptual field of bold leadership, a real semantic chain though not a direct gloss match.
Woman giving birth vs Eileithyia (goddess of childbirth) - semantic match via childbirth

Arabic البرخاش: uproar, confusion, clamor. Greek βραχέσι: dative plural of βραχύς (short/brief). The Arabic mafahim of 'chaotic noise' vs. Greek grammatical form — phonological match br-kh-sh but semantic mismatch. However βραχύς also means 'quick/brief' which can link to the sudden outburst quality.

Arabic البلند: root of henna plant. Greek Βλάνδα: city in Basilicata, Italy. The toponym Blanda may derive from a Mediterranean plant name (henna-like coastal plant). Consonant match b-l-n-d. Plausible botanical-toponym link.
